reasoning: The question asks to compare the daily average temperature in Abbeville on October 26 with three dates later in the winter: Nov 2, Nov 25, and Jan 24. Average temperatures decrease steadily over this period in northern France. The approximate historical climatological means are 11.2, 10.3, 7.2, and 4.9 degrees Celsius respectively. Assuming a similar day-to-day temperature variability throughout autumn and winter (standard deviation around 3.5 to 5.0 degrees), we can model the temperature difference between these dates as a normal distribution with the respective difference in means and a variance roughly equal to the sum of the variances. This normal approximation yields marginal probabilities of about 0.42, 0.24, and 0.15 for the later dates to be strictly warmer than Oct 26. Since the current actual weather of Oct 26 cannot be verified past the knowledge cutoff constraint using real-time search, relying on the marginalized seasonal climatology provides the mathematically correct Bayesian forecast.
